title: saxifraga longifolia subsp. lantoscana
intro: Saxifraga longifolia subsp. lantoscana, also known as the alpine saxifrage, is a sub-species of perennial flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifraga genus. It is native to high-altitude mountainous regions in Europe, where it grows in rocky and alpine areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and inflammation. The aerial parts of the plant can be used to prepare teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including kidney and urinary problems, and to promote overall well-being.
